title: Damper Leakage Concern
summary: Low-leakage dampers cost more and require larger actuators because of friction of the seals in the closed position; however, the energy savings offset the extra cost.
evidence_quote: Low-leakage dampers cost more and require larger actuators because of friction of the seals in the closed position; however, the energy savings offset the extra cost.

title: Damper Actuator Types
summary: Electric damper actuators can be proportional or two-position. They can be spring return or nonspring.
evidence_quote: Electric damper actuators can be proportional or two-position. They can be spring return or nonspring.

title: Damper Mounting Methods
summary: The most common method of mounting electric actuators is directly over the damper shaft (direct coupling) with no external linkage.
evidence_quote: The most common method of mounting electric actuators is directly over the damper shaft (direct coupling) with no external linkage.

title: Pneumatic Positive Positioners
summary: Where accurate positioning of a modulating pneumatic damper or valve is required, use positive positioners.
evidence_quote: Where accurate positioning of a modulating pneumatic damper or valve is required, use positive positioners.

title: Compressibility Velocity Threshold
summary: Incompressible flow analysis is satisfactory for gases and vapors at velocities below about 20 to 40 m/s, except in long conduits.
evidence_quote: incompressible flow analysis is satisfactory for gases and vapors at velocities below about 20 to 40 m/s, except in long conduits.

title: Pitot Tube Compressibility Influence
summary: When the upstream Mach number exceeds 0.2, the difference is significant. Thus, a pitot tube in air is influenced by compressibility at velocities over about 66 m/s.
evidence_quote: When the upstream Mach number exceeds 0.2, the difference is significant. Thus, a pitot tube in air is influenced by compressibility at velocities over about 66 m/s.

title: Ratio of Specific Heats for Air
summary: k, the ratio of specific heats at constant pressure and volume, is 1.4 for air and diatomic gases.
evidence_quote: k, the ratio of specific heats at constant pressure and volume, is 1.4 for air and diatomic gases.

title: Human Thermal Tolerance - Mean Skin Temperature
summary: The critical subjective tolerance limit (without numbing) for mean skin temperature appears to be about 25°C.
evidence_quote: The critical subjective tolerance limit (without numbing) for mean skin temperature appears to be about 25°C.

title: Human Thermal Tolerance - Hand Skin Temperature
summary: A hand-skin temperature of 20°C causes a report of uncomfortably cold; 15°C, extremely cold; and 5°C, painful.
evidence_quote: A hand-skin temperature of 20°C causes a report of uncomfortably cold; 15°C, extremely cold; and 5°C, painful.

title: Lower Limit for Outdoor Activity
summary: An ambient temperature of –35°C is the lower limit for useful outdoor activity, even with adequate insulative clothing.
evidence_quote: An ambient temperature of –35°C is the lower limit for useful outdoor activity, even with adequate insulative clothing.

title: Shivering Heat Production
summary: Ultimately violent, whole-body shivering causes maximum heat production of about 6 times resting levels, rendering the individual totally ineffective.
evidence_quote: Ultimately violent, whole-body shivering causes maximum heat production of about 6 times resting levels, rendering the individual totally ineffective.

title: Bioaerosol Sampling Methods
summary: Culture plate impactors, including multiple- and single-stage devices as well as slit-to-agar samplers, are most useful in office environments where low concentrations of bacteria and fungi are expected.
evidence_quote: Culture plate impactors, including multiple- and single-stage devices as well as slit-to-agar samplers, are most useful in office environments where low concentrations of bacteria and fungi are expected.

title: PCR Testing Advantage
summary: Traditional culturing can take anywhere from 3 to 7 days, whereas real-time PCR can often be run in 10 to 24 h.
evidence_quote: Traditional culturing can take anywhere from 3 to 7 days, whereas real-time PCR can often be run in 10 to 24 h.

title: Particulate Matter Control Methods
summary: Control of airborne particulate levels may be achieved by one of four methods: Reduction of source emissions, Capture of emissions at the source using local exhaust, Dilution using mechanical ventilation, Removal from ventilation air by filtration.
evidence_quote:
Control of airborne particulate levels may be achieved by one of four methods:
– Reduction of source emissions
– Capture of emissions at the source using local exhaust
– Dilution using mechanical ventilation
– Removal from ventilation air by filtration

title: Gas vs. Vapor Definition
summary: Gas is the correct term for describing any pure substance or mixture that naturally exists in the gaseous state at normal atmospheric conditions. Vapor is used to describe a substance in the gaseous state whose natural state is a liquid or solid at normal atmospheric conditions.
evidence_quote: Gas is the correct term for describing any pure substance or mixture that naturally exists in the gaseous state at normal atmospheric conditions. Vapor is used to describe a substance in the gaseous state whose natural state is a liquid or solid at normal atmospheric conditions.
